member.office_address: 2132 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3212

form_analysis.form_header_text: FY27 Programmatic and Language Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: This form is for submissions to the Office of Congressman Jerrold Nadler (NY-12). Submissions are due by Friday, March 6, 2026, at 11:59pm (EST). If you have any edits or amendments to your submission that is already submitted, please email Matthew.Steinbaum@mail.house.gov. If you are submitting multiple requests, please separate each request as a new submission form. When you submit this form, it will not automatically collect your details like name and email address unless you provide it yourself.

sections.description: Information about the entity making the request.

fields.label: Type of Entity
fields.help_text: E.g. non-profit, university, local government, etc.

sections.description: Information regarding the specific appropriations request.

fields.label: What Bill Is Your Request For
options: Labor, Health and Human Services, Education (LHHS)
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, Related Agencies (MilCon-VA)
National Security, State, and Related Programs (NSSRP/SFOPs)
fields.rationale: NASA's Science Mission Directorate is funded under the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies (CJS) Appropriations Subcommittee.

fields.label: Agency / Department
fields.rationale: As specified in the reference values for this request.

fields.label: Account for Request Submission
fields.rationale: This identifies the top-level account for the Science Mission Directorate within the NASA budget structure.

fields.label: Program / Sub-Account (if needed)
fields.rationale: This specifies the primary directorate within the Science account being funded.

fields.label: Request Title
fields.rationale: Standard title used for programmatic funding requests for this account.

fields.label: Type of Request
fields.help_text: A programmatic request is a request to fund a general federal agency or program at a specified level. A language request is a request for bill or report language that does not direct funding to a particular entity but encourages, urges, or directs action.
fields.rationale: This request is to fund the NASA Science Mission Directorate at a specific level ($9 billion), which is a programmatic request.

fields.label: Please share drafted language below (if applicable)
fields.draft_value:
* Provide $9,000,000,000 for the NASA Science Mission Directorate (SMD) in the Science account.
* This funding level restores program stability following FY2026 disruptions and ensures the U.S. maintains global leadership in planetary exploration.
* Directs NASA to align resources with the National Academies' Decadal Survey recommendations.
fields.rationale: The value reflects the requested $9 billion programmatic funding level for the SMD as specified in the ask_config and reference context.

sections.description: Funding amounts and justifications for the request.

fields.label: Your Requested FY27 Amount
fields.rationale: This matches the standard funding ask provided in the reference context.

fields.label: Amount Included in the President's FY27 Budget
fields.help_text: If unknown, please type 'UNSURE' / If this doesn't apply to your request, please type 'N/A'. If the President's FY27 Budget is not released yet, please use FY26 request amount.
fields.draft_value: Currently unknown (President's Budget Request has not been released)
fields.rationale: Since the FY27 President's Budget Request is unknown, the form instructions specify using the FY26 request amount ($3.908 billion).

fields.label: President's Budget Line #
fields.help_text: If unknown, please type 'UNSURE' / If this doesn't apply to your request, please type 'N/A'. If the President's FY27 Budget is not released yet, please use FY26 line #.
fields.rationale: The specific budget line number for the upcoming or prior year is not provided in the reference context.

fields.label: Prior Enacted Amounts (FY26, FY25, FY24)
fields.help_text: This is for ongoing programmatic requests. Please provide specific dollar amounts for historical fiscal years.
fields.rationale: Enacted amounts are taken directly from the provided SMD Topline Funding History. FY24 is not provided in the context.

fields.label: Purpose & Impact
fields.help_text: Describe the purpose and impact of language or programmatic request. How will this request help your organization, New York, and/or the nation?
fields.draft_value:
* Directly supports $11.6M+ in recent NASA Science obligations in NY-12, including major grants to the American Museum of Natural History ($16.3M cumulative) and CUNY ($3.5M cumulative).
* Protects a highly skilled local STEM workforce and sustains a 3:1 economic return on federal investment in aerospace and research sectors.
* Counteracts rapid expansion of China's space science program to ensure the U.S. continues to set the norms for lunar and deep-space exploration.
* Advances non-partisan scientific priorities that yield practical benefits in weather forecasting, climate monitoring, and planetary defense for all Americans.
fields.rationale: Utilizes specific local spending data for AMNH and CUNY in NY-12 and integrates strategic arguments regarding economic ROI and international competition from the advocacy guide.

sections.description: Optional section for additional notes and reference materials.

fields.label: Notes for Staff
fields.help_text: If you have any links/documents to share, please past those links here.
fields.draft_value:
* This request aligns with the bipartisan House letter led by Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) to stabilize NASA Science funding.
* The $9 billion request is essential to recover from the FY2026 budget cycle which resulted in significant civil servant and contractor job losses.
* Funding levels are calibrated to meet the peer-reviewed consensus priorities of the National Academies' Decadal Surveys.
fields.rationale: References the Bacon-Chu letter and the context of FY2026 funding restoration provided in the reference values.

top_grants.description: M dwarfs are the most common and longest-lived stars in the galaxy  and they host some of the most exciting planetary systems discovered so far. The Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS) and the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) are targeting

top_grants.recipient_name: The American Museum of Natural History
top_grants.description: Execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress.   Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond.   Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines   develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Executing a balanced science program for Moon, Mars, and applied research.
